nativeRange.habitat: Inshore reef-associated benthic environments; commonly found on coral reefs and in tide pools at depths from 0 to 85 meters.

diet.notes: Generally fed meaty foods such as fresh or frozen shrimp, squid, scallop, and marine fish flesh. Target feeding 2–3 times per week has been reported as effective. In the wild, has been documented feeding on bottom invertebrates and small fish.

goodWith: other large fish too big to swallow
avoidWith: small fish that fit in its mouth

summary: A large benthic elasmobranch from Indo-Pacific reefs, commonly considered expert-level due to its size (reaching around 100–130 cm), specialized substrate needs, and sensitivity to copper-based medications. Best suited to very large, species-appropriate systems.
careNotes:
The Marbled Bamboo Cat Shark (Chiloscyllium punctatum) is a nocturnal, bottom-dwelling shark that tends to hide during daylight hours and becomes active at night. It generally requires a soft sand substrate, as coarser materials have been known to cause abrasion injuries to the ventral surface which can lead to infection. Caves or darkened areas are advisable so it can shelter during the day.

Adult size: FishBase records a maximum total length of 132 cm in the wild; hobby sources commonly cite 40–41 inches (approximately 102–104 cm) as a practical adult size in the trade. The averaged maximum used here is approximately 113 cm.

Minimum tank volume: Sources disagree — AquariumDomain's quick-facts section states 350 gallons (approximately 1,325 L) while AquariumDomain's article body and FreshMarine both state 180 gallons (approximately 681 L). The averaged figure used here is approximately 896 L (about 237 US gallons); most hobbyists and experts tend to recommend the larger end of this range given the shark's eventual adult size. This field is flagged needs_review.

Temperament: Two of three sources (LiveAquaria and FreshMarine) rate this species as aggressive; AquariumDomain rates it semi-aggressive. The majority rating of aggressive is applied here.

Elasmobranchs including this species are known to be highly sensitive to copper-based medications — copper treatments should be avoided entirely.

This species is oviparous (egg-laying). Captive breeding has been documented rarely; eggs have been sold in the trade. It has been reported to survive up to 12 hours out of water, though this should not be treated as routine handling guidance.

Temperature notes: FishBase reports a preferred range of 24.6–29.1°C based on wild occurrence data; hobby sources report 22.2–26.7°C (72–80°F). The averaged range of 23.0–27.1°C is used here.

Commonly traded under multiple names: Bamboo Shark, Brownbanded Bamboo Shark, Brown-banded Catshark, Marble Cat Shark, and Blackbanded Cat Shark are all trade names for this species.
breedingNotes: Oviparous — females deposit egg cases in the environment. Eggs have occasionally appeared in the aquarium trade (sold separately). Captive breeding has been reported in large public aquarium exhibits and rarely in home aquaria. Detailed captive breeding protocols are not well-documented in hobbyist literature.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Collections page confirmed care level (Expert Only), temperament (Aggressive), and trade listing. Species-specific detail page not directly accessible; supplemented with additional hobbyist sources.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed scientific name, taxonomy (family Hemiscylliidae, order Orectolobiformes), native range (Indo-West Pacific, India to Japan/Australia), wild max size 132 cm, preferred temperature 24.6–29.1°C, reef-associated habitat 0–85 m depth, oviparous reproduction.

additional.name: AquariumDomain
additional.notes: Provided adult size (40 in / 101.6 cm), temp 72–80°F (22.2–26.7°C), pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, care level Expert, temperament semi-aggressive, carnivore diet. Conflicting tank size: quick facts state 350 gal (1,325 L); article text states 180 gal (681 L).

additional.name: FreshMarine
additional.notes: Provided adult size (40.9 in / 103.9 cm), temp 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C), pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, min tank 180 gal (681 L), care level Expert Only, temperament Aggressive, carnivore diet. Used to cross-verify high-stakes parameters.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous; egg cases have been sold in the trade and hatched in aquaria. True captive breeding in home tanks is rarely if ever reported; breeding is considered not practical for home aquarists.
